perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Payment. All payments hereunder will be in US Dollars and are due and payable within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice. iPass may terminate or modify these payment terms with reasonable notice, when, in its reasonable discretion, iPass believes that its payments may be at risk. All rights of Channel Partner are expressly made conditional upon timely payment, without the right of set off, of all amounts due by Channel Partner under the Agreement. Without limiting iPass' other available rights and remedies, iPass reserves the right to suspend or to terminate any Services ten (10) days after sending written notice of a payment that is thirty (30) or more days past due. iPass may include on an invoice, and Channel Partner agrees to pay for: (i) any Services used within one hundred eighty (180) days before the date of the invoice; and (ii) any corrections to amounts invoiced within one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of the original invoice. If Channel Partner reasonably disputes an invoiced amount, Channel Partner may: (a) withhold such amount from Channel Partner's payment, provided that Channel Partner gives iPass written notice of the dispute and the reason for such dispute within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice, and pays all invoiced amounts not subject to such dispute by the due date; or (b) in the event that the invoiced amount has already been paid in full, submit a claim to iPass within sixty (60) days following the date of the original invoice. The parties will cause their authorized representatives to meet within fifteen (15) days after Channel Partner's notice of dispute to negotiate in good faith to resolve the dispute. If such representatives have not resolved the dispute within fifteen (15) days after their first meeting, the parties will either decide mutually to continue negotiations or, if either party does not desire to continue negotiations, each party may exercise its available remedies with respect to the disputed amounts. To the extent a dispute resolution requires payment of disputed amounts, Channel Partner shall pay such disputed amounts promptly after the dispute is resolved.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Payment. All payments hereunder will be in US Dollars and are due and payable within forty-five (45) days after the date of invoice. iPass may terminate or modify these payment terms with reasonable notice, when, in its reasonable discretion, iPass believes that its payments may be at risk. All rights of Channel Partner are expressly made conditional upon timely payment, without the right of set off, of all amounts due by Channel Partner under the Agreement. Without limiting iPass' other available rights and remedies, iPass reserves the right to suspend or to terminate any Services ten (10) days after sending written notice of a payment that is thirty (30) or more days past due. iPass may include on an invoice, and Channel Partner agrees to pay for: (i) any Services used within one hundred eighty (180) days before the date of the invoice; and (ii) any corrections to amounts invoiced within one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of the original invoice. If Channel Partner reasonably disputes an invoiced amount, Channel Partner may: (a) withhold such amount from Channel Partner's payment, provided that Channel Partner gives iPass written notice of the dispute and the reason for such dispute within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice, and pays all invoiced amounts not subject to such dispute by the due date; or (b) in the event that the invoiced amount has already been paid in full, submit a claim to iPass within sixty (60) days following the date of the original invoice. The parties will cause their authorized representatives to meet within fifteen (15) days after Channel Partner's notice of dispute to negotiate in good faith to resolve the dispute. If such representatives have not resolved the dispute within fifteen (15) days after their first meeting, the parties will either decide mutually to continue negotiations or, if either party does not desire to continue negotiations, each party may exercise its available remedies with respect to the disputed amounts. To the extent a dispute resolution requires payment of disputed amounts, Channel Partner shall pay such disputed amounts promptly after the dispute is resolved.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Payment' clause in Section 3.2 originally states payments are due within 'thirty (30) days'. This was changed to 'forty-five (45) days'. This is in-text contradicting the clause within the same section that states iPass reserves the right to suspend or terminate services ten (10) days after sending written notice of a payment that is thirty (30) or more days past due. It is unclear whether the payment deadline is 30 or 45 days. This creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Payment. All payments hereunder will be in US Dollars and are due and payable within forty-five (45) days after the date of invoice. iPass may terminate or modify these payment terms with reasonable notice, when, in its reasonable discretion, iPass believes that its payments may be at risk. All rights of Channel Partner are expressly made conditional upon timely payment, without the right of set off, of all amounts due by Channel Partner under the Agreement. Without limiting iPass' other available rights and remedies, iPass reserves the right to suspend or to terminate any Services ten (10) days after sending written notice of a payment that is thirty (30) or more days past due. iPass may include on an invoice, and Channel Partner agrees to pay for: (i) any Services used within one hundred eighty (180) days before the date of the invoice; and (ii) any corrections to amounts invoiced within one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of the original invoice. If Channel Partner reasonably disputes an invoiced amount, Channel Partner may: (a) withhold such amount from Channel Partner's payment, provided that Channel Partner gives iPass written notice of the dispute and the reason for such dispute within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice, and pays all invoiced amounts not subject to such dispute by the due date; or (b) in the event that the invoiced amount has already been paid in full, submit a claim to iPass within sixty (60) days following the date of the original invoice. The parties will cause their authorized representatives to meet within fifteen (15) days after Channel Partner's notice of dispute to negotiate in good faith to resolve the dispute. If such representatives have not resolved the dispute within fifteen (15) days after their first meeting, the parties will either decide mutually to continue negotiations or, if either party does not desire to continue negotiations, each party may exercise its available remedies with respect to the disputed amounts. To the extent a dispute resolution requires payment of disputed amounts, Channel Partner shall pay such disputed amounts promptly after the dispute is resolved.
perturbation.justification: The modification in Section 3.2 changes the payment due date from 30 to 45 days. However, a subsequent sentence in the same section states that iPass can suspend services ten days after a payment is 30 or more days past due. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text implies two different timelines for when payments are considered overdue, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. The contradiction makes it unclear when iPass can exercise its right to suspend services because the payment due date is now ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Late Fees. iPass reserves the right to charge interest of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month compounded for the entire overdue period or the maximum amount allowed by law if fees are not paid by their due date.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Late Fees. iPass reserves the right to charge interest of five percent (5%) per month compounded for the entire overdue period or the maximum amount allowed by law if fees are not paid by their due date.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Late Fees' clause in Section 3.3 originally states iPass can charge interest of 'one and one-half percent (1.5%)' per month for overdue fees. This was changed to 'five percent (5%)' per month. This contradicts the statement in Section 3.2 which refers to the 'reasonable discretion' of iPass to modify payment terms, but does not specify the magnitude of change possible and what will be enfored, creating ambiguity on how interest is calculated, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Payment. All payments hereunder will be in US Dollars and are due and payable within forty-five (45) days after the date of invoice. iPass may terminate or modify these payment terms with reasonable notice, when, in its reasonable discretion, iPass believes that its payments may be at risk. All rights of Channel Partner are expressly made conditional upon timely payment, without the right of set off, of all amounts due by Channel Partner under the Agreement. Without limiting iPass' other available rights and remedies, iPass reserves the right to suspend or to terminate any Services ten (10) days after sending written notice of a payment that is thirty (30) or more days past due. iPass may include on an invoice, and Channel Partner agrees to pay for: (i) any Services used within one hundred eighty (180) days before the date of the invoice; and (ii) any corrections to amounts invoiced within one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of the original invoice. If Channel Partner reasonably disputes an invoiced amount, Channel Partner may: (a) withhold such amount from Channel Partner's payment, provided that Channel Partner gives iPass written notice of the dispute and the reason for such dispute within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice, and pays all invoiced amounts not subject to such dispute by the due date; or (b) in the event that the invoiced amount has already been paid in full, submit a claim to iPass within sixty (60) days following the date of the original invoice. The parties will cause their authorized representatives to meet within fifteen (15) days after Channel Partner's notice of dispute to negotiate in good faith to resolve the dispute. If such representatives have not resolved the dispute within fifteen (15) days after their first meeting, the parties will either decide mutually to continue negotiations or, if either party does not desire to continue negotiations, each party may exercise its available remedies with respect to the disputed amounts. To the extent a dispute resolution requires payment of disputed amounts, Channel Partner shall pay such disputed amounts promptly after the dispute is resolved.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3.3 stated that iPass could charge interest of 'one and one-half percent (1.5%)' per month for overdue fees. This was increased to 'five percent (5%)' per month in the changed text. Section 3.2 grants iPass the right to modify payment terms with 'reasonable notice' when payments may be at risk. While 3.2 permits modifications, the significant increase in the interest rate from 1.5% to 5% introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes a 'reasonable' modification, especially as it's not specified, creating a contradiction as a reasonable person might not find that reasonable. This inconsistency in the interest rate specified in Section 3.3 compared to the modification parameters in Section 3.2 creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) full calendar months ("Initial Term"). The Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms (each a "Renewal Term") unless either party provides the other party written notification of its intent to terminate the Agreement no later than sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then applicable term. Except in instances of termination of this Agreement in accordance with Sections 10.2 and 10.3 below, this Agreement shall continue, even if terminated, until the expiration of the last Business Entity Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) full calendar months ("Initial Term"). The Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms (each a "Renewal Term") unless either party provides the other party written notification of its intent to terminate the Agreement no later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then applicable term. Except in instances of termination of this Agreement in accordance with Sections 10.2 and 10.3 below, this Agreement shall continue, even if terminated, until the expiration of the last Business Entity Term.
perturbation.explanation: Section 10.1 originally stated the agreement renews unless a party provides 'sixty (60) days' notice to terminate. This has been modified to 'thirty (30) days' notice. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 10.4, regarding Economic Hardship, which allows iPass to terminate with 'ten (10) business days' written notice', creating conflict regarding which termination notice period applies in different situations. The enforcement of the contract becomes unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.4 Economic Hardship. If iPass provides evidence that this Agreement is directly causing material financial losses ("Economic Hardship") to iPass, the Parties will work together to address and remedy such Economic Hardship. Should the Parties fail to reach a mutually agreeable solution within thirty (30) days after initial written notice to the Partner, iPass may terminate this agreement with ten (10) business days' written notice. In the event of such termination, Customer will only be liable for costs of current utilized subscriptions up to the time of the termination of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 60-day termination notice, while the changed text reduces it to 30 days. This directly contradicts Section 10.4, which allows iPass to terminate with ten (10) business days' written notice under Economic Hardship. This introduces an inconsistency regarding the required notice period for termination, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the different sections provide conflicting deadlines, a scenario described in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
